title: Husbands And Knives

jokes.text: The pilgrims were not illegal aliens
jokes.punchline: Anachronistic application of modern immigration terminology to pilgrims
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons chalkboard gag format with political edge - solid but not exceptional

jokes.text: Lame, lame, lame, lame, have it, lame, Superman Dies, Aquaman Dies, Casper Dies, Caveman Robin, Black Robin, Born-Again Robin
jokes.setup: Bart dismissing comic books as lame
jokes.punchline: Absurd comic book titles, especially 'Born-Again Robin'
jokes.explanation: Good satire of comic book death/variant culture with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: Nice work, Doctor Boo-Who. Your tears have smudged Wolverine's iconic sideburns. Hence, you must buy this comic. And the cost of your innocent accident is.... $25, please.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy being mean about smudged comic
jokes.punchline: Forcing purchase for accidental damage at inflated price
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy character moment - his condescending nature and exploitation tactics

jokes.text: But that's the money Yaya Sofia gave me for Greek Orthodox Easter. I hate when they tell me things about themselves.
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's callous dismissal of personal information
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character writing - CBG's sociopathy revealed through perfect line delivery

jokes.text: Maybe they sell comics at the dry cleaners. No? Perhaps they sell comics at the mattress store No! Perhaps you could buy your comics at that new comic bookstore across the street?
jokes.punchline: Final suggestion reveals actual threat to his business
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with ironic reversal - his sarcasm backfires

jokes.text: Philip K. Dick. It can't be. It's as if Superman moved to Gotham City. Which he did, in World's Finest Comics n°24.
jokes.setup: CBG making dramatic comparison to fictional scenario
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction with obscure comic reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect CBG pedantry - even his metaphors get fact-checked by himself

jokes.text: That was an imaginary story dreamt by Jimmy Olsen after he was kicked in the head by Supergirl's horse, Comet. It never really happened. None of these things ever really happened. Get out of my store.
jokes.punchline: Existential crisis followed by customer ejection
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from pedantry to existential breakdown - absurd comic lore detail makes it funnier

jokes.text: Hey what up, you guys? How g-g-g-goes it? My name's Milo, and this is Coolsville.
jokes.setup: New cool comic store owner introduction
jokes.punchline: Trying-too-hard coolness with awkward stutter
jokes.explanation: Solid character introduction showing forced coolness vs CBG's authentic nerdiness

jokes.text: Down low. Go long. I got prawn. Mmm. I got miso. Mmm. I got dolphin.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly weird candy flavors ending with dolphin
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in candy flavors from normal to disturbing

jokes.text: Asterix! Tintin! I heard these only existed in high school French classes.
jokes.punchline: Reference to their limited availability in American education
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa line showing her academic awareness and cultural observation

jokes.text: Come on, Snowy, we must save the Belgian ambassador from the Black Orchid Gang. What's that? A sound of ignition? Zut, alors! This castle is actually a four-stage rocket and it's headed straight for the Pompidou Center.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous over-the-top Tintin adventure parody
jokes.explanation: Good parody of European adventure comics with French expressions and absurd plot

jokes.text: Hey, no worries, little lady. These books are meant to be read and enjoyed, not hoarded and then sold when you get divorced.
jokes.punchline: Milo's casual reference to divorce while being 'cool'
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Milo's laid-back attitude and CBG's attitude, with dark adult reference

jokes.text: All right, I get it. You're cool, you're not 'mainstream,' You wear a porkpie hat. Mmm... porkpie.
jokes.setup: Bart challenging the new cool store owner
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by food association with hat name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - his attention span derailed by food thoughts

jokes.text: Who's stronger? The Thung or The Mulk? And show your work.
jokes.punchline: Obvious parody names of The Thing and The Hulk
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective parody names, 'show your work' adds to the test format

jokes.text: Well, the Mulk kicked a tidal wave into the sun, whereas... The Thung gave a piggyback ride to the 1985 Chicago Bears.
jokes.punchline: Absurd comparison between cosmic and oddly specific feats
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd comparison - cosmic vs oddly specific team-based feat

jokes.text: I was in such a bad relationship with my ex-Comic Book Guy, I'd forgotten how good it could be.
jokes.punchline: Framing customer service as romantic relationship
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor comparing customer service to romantic relationships

jokes.text: Look, Maggie, I'm Wonder Woman. Oh. I've lost my perfect 26-26-26 figure. Never compare yourself to a standee.
jokes.setup: Marge comparing herself to Wonder Woman cutout
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical advice about unrealistic standards
jokes.explanation: Good body image joke with Homer providing unexpectedly sensible advice

jokes.text: Hey, you're way skinnier than a lot of superheroes. Girthquake, Flaberella. Kearney's Mom.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to make Marge feel better about her figure
jokes.punchline: Made-up fat superhero names escalating to real person
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from silly superhero names to local person - 'Flaberella' is particularly clever

jokes.text: Shut up! Her depression medication makes her bloated. She's depressed 'cause you're so lame. Shut up!
jokes.punchline: Dark revelation about depression and medication
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation typical of bullies revealing too much personal info

jokes.text: Ooh, I'd better join a gym before I go from hippy to hippo. I wish my Mom said cute things like that. She can't 'cause she's depressed. Shut up.
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's dark family revelation
jokes.explanation: Continuation of depression theme with Marge's pun contrasted against family dysfunction

jokes.text: Alan Moore, Art Spiegelman. Oh, Dan Clowes. I really identified with the girls in Ghost World. They made me feel like I wasn't so alone.
jokes.punchline: Sincere teenage connection to alternative comics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - earnest appreciation for serious graphic novels

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, whatever. Do you know anyone who works at Batman? 'Cause I really want to draw Batman. I'm awesome at utility belts. Check these out. This is where the Batman keeps his money in case he has to take the bus.
jokes.setup: Bart dismissing Lisa's serious comic talk
jokes.punchline: Practical utility belt design for public transportation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd practicality - Batman needing bus fare is wonderfully mundane

jokes.text: Alan Moore, you wrote my favorite issues of Radioactive Man. Oh, really. So you'd like that I made your favorite superhero a heroin-addicted jazz critic who's not radioactive? I don't read the words. I just like when he punches people.
jokes.punchline: Moore's dark deconstruction vs Bart's simple action preference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of Alan Moore's dark reimaginings vs typical fan expectations

jokes.text: Mr. Moore, will you sign my DVD of Watchmen Babies? Which of the babies is your favorite?
jokes.punchline: Commercialized baby version of dark adult comic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - 'Watchmen Babies' is brilliant absurd commercialization satire

jokes.text: You see what those bloody corporations do? They take your ideas and they suck them! Suck them like leeches until they've gotten every last drop of the marrow from your bones!
jokes.punchline: Overwrought metaphor mixing leeches and bone marrow
jokes.explanation: Good parody of Alan Moore's famous anti-corporate rants with mixed metaphor

jokes.text: Hey, Teacup, why don't you chill out? Very well. Oh, little Lulu, I love you-lu, just the same.
jokes.setup: Milo telling angry Moore to chill
jokes.punchline: Moore immediately switching to gentle children's rhyme
jokes.explanation: Great character flip showing Moore's gentler side, unexpected tonal shift

jokes.text: Attention, comic book aficionados! This man is not one of us! He has a girlfriend! My name's Strawberry. My purse is a lunchbox.
jokes.setup: CBG declaring Milo an outsider for having girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Strawberry's quirky self-introduction
jokes.explanation: Good nerd gatekeeping joke with quirky girlfriend detail

jokes.text: You would sell weapons of the Orient to children? That is weak.
jokes.setup: CBG announcing ninja weapons for sale
jokes.punchline: Moore's understated moral objection
jokes.explanation: Moore's casual condemnation of selling weapons to children is perfectly deadpan

jokes.text: Oh, no! The store's in trouble! League of Extraordinary Freelancers, activate! Maus is in the house!
jokes.punchline: Art Spiegelman using his famous work title as battle cry
jokes.explanation: Clever use of graphic novel title as superhero battle cry

jokes.text: How do ya like this punch line?
jokes.punchline: Dan Clowes using 'punch line' literally while punching
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun combining comedy terminology with physical action

jokes.text: We've got Tummy Tone with Sasha, Power Bounce with Zach D., Zen Abs with Zach G., and you've just gotta try Mommy and Me kickboxing!
jokes.punchline: Escalating to violent mother-child bonding activity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to absurd family fitness class combination

jokes.text: Who thought walking could be so difficult? Well, I'm sure everyone else is having trouble, too.
jokes.punchline: Implied visual of everyone else doing fine
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge self-delusion moment - visual comedy of her struggling alone

jokes.text: Marge, you missed a spot.
jokes.punchline: Being treated as cleaning staff
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable class-based humor showing Marge's social position

jokes.text: Rules: no men, no cell phones, no mirrors, no shame. Marge, I wish you well, but why would women want to go to a gym if there were no men there watching them and judging them?
jokes.punchline: Patty's cynical take on women's workout motivations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty cynicism about gender dynamics and fitness culture

jokes.text: why would women want to go to a gym if there were no men there watching them and judging them?
jokes.punchline: Selma missing the point entirely about male judgment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selma's internalized need for male validation

jokes.text: We're gonna be rich! We can finally start a family! We have a family. A better one!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about Marge's business success
jokes.punchline: Homer suggesting family replacement
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - casual dismissal of existing family for upgrade

jokes.text: Finally, an exercise bike for women of a certain age Jurassic!
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating prehistoric age reference
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective self-deprecating age humor with prehistoric twist

jokes.text: I can't believe the labor board is shutting me down! You lock your workers in at night. It's so they can't tell their stories!
jokes.setup: Factory being shut down for violations
jokes.punchline: Owner admitting to imprisoning workers casually
jokes.explanation: Dark labor exploitation humor with casual admission of guilt

jokes.text: Mr. Krusty came to my village. He said he would marry me. No ring! Just fill apple pies all day!
jokes.punchline: Krusty's false marriage promises for labor exploitation
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective satire of corporate labor exploitation with Krusty twist

jokes.text: When is Straightman gonna pop the question?
jokes.punchline: Partner name 'Straightman' as comedy term reference
jokes.explanation: Decent pun using comedy terminology as character name

jokes.text: You're all getting German cuckoo clocks! And you're getting a cuckoo clock!
jokes.punchline: Parody of Oprah's famous car giveaway with weird gift
jokes.explanation: Standard parody of famous TV moment with absurd gift substitution

jokes.text: The TV remote isn't nailed on! My whole life, I've never been in a hotel that trusted me.
jokes.punchline: Revelation about his hotel experiences and trustworthiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer observation revealing his questionable past behavior with hotels

jokes.text: Hors d'oeuvres, big fancy desserts... and my wife is paying for everything. Now I know why pimps are so happy.
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying fancy party on Marge's dime
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to a pimp
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Homer making inappropriate comparison about living off wife's income

jokes.text: Yep, nothing beats living on 'wife-support.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of living off wives' income
jokes.punchline: Reversing 'wife support' concept
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay reversing traditional support roles

jokes.text: My wife invented 'Skipills', she mixed vitamins with Alka-Seltzer and pretended it was medicine. I pay ten dollars a tube for those! That's why I drive a Bentley.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake medicine that makes millions
jokes.explanation: Good satire of wellness industry scams with absurd product name

jokes.text: Well, you'd better decide soon. 'Cause I have a feeling your wife will also be getting a 'new model.' Yeah, she'll be 'trading up.' And they're not talking about cars. Are you sure they're not talking about cars? Because those are car words.
jokes.setup: Husbands warning Homer about wife leaving
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of car metaphors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing obvious subtext by focusing on literal car terminology

jokes.text: Marge won't dump me. I'm the anchor that keeps her weighed down.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describing himself as burden
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer self-own - meant as defense but reveals he knows he's deadweight

jokes.text: Here's how you know she's really about to go: your wife seems happy and full of life. That will never happen!
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident assertion Marge will never be happy
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective Homer logic - confident his wife will stay miserable

jokes.text: A makeover! Oh, my God! The prophecy is being fulfilled.
jokes.punchline: Treating warnings as biblical prophecy
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier warnings with Homer's dramatic interpretation

jokes.text: Well, the first inspirational speech of the day was by the woman who climbed Mount Everest and got everyone else killed. Hey, the networks have different channels in this city. That's okay. I don't care that you don't care.
jokes.setup: Marge sharing her day, Homer distracted by TV
jokes.punchline: Dark inspirational speaker detail and Homer's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about motivational speaker plus relationship deterioration

jokes.text: Are you happy and full of life? I sure am!
jokes.setup: Homer directly testing the warning signs
jokes.punchline: Marge confirming worst fears
jokes.explanation: Direct callback confirming the prophecy - effective escalation

jokes.text: Really? But I'm so used to my old one. Old one? They're convincing Marge to dump me! Oh, it's easy. I get a new one every two years. From Italy! You would love a big black one.
jokes.punchline: Homer misinterpreting as husband replacement talk
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Homer's paranoia making innocent talk seem threatening

jokes.text: If Marge leaves, I'll have nothing except my many friends and half the fortune she is now making and will continue to make.
jokes.punchline: Listing substantial benefits he'd actually receive
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal - Homer's 'tragedy' would actually benefit him greatly

jokes.text: I used to look like this. What's your secret? Oh, there's no secret. Just hard work.... exercise two hours a day... keep up with the latest fashions... and of course, cut out all the fatty foods and alcohol. Got it.
jokes.setup: Homer learning how to stay attractive
jokes.punchline: List of everything Homer would hate doing
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's character - listing everything he'd never do

jokes.text: Please, Doc. I know I'm not the greatest-looking guy in the world, but I took care of my family, and that used to be enough. But not anymore.
jokes.punchline: Sincere emotional vulnerability rare for Homer
jokes.explanation: Rare sincere Homer moment that works as character development

jokes.text: I guess, considering all the training you've received, this is quite reasonable. I've never seen anything so reasonable! It's the bargain of a lifetime! And... Dad, are you okay? I see food on your plate instead of blurring motions.
jokes.punchline: Bill so expensive it knocks him out, followed by eating observation
jokes.explanation: Good escalating medical bill joke followed by Bart's eating speed observation

jokes.text: I see food on your plate instead of blurring motions.
jokes.setup: Bart noticing something different about Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer eating slowly enough to see individual food items
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about Homer's usual eating speed creating visual blur

jokes.text: Kids, your daddy underwent a special procedure so he can be more attractive to your mother. You had your hot dog plumped? No, I had my stomach stapled!
jokes.punchline: Bart's assumption about cosmetic enhancement
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Bart assuming male enhancement surgery

jokes.text: You had your hot dog plumped? No, I had my stomach stapled!
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding plastic surgery for food enhancement
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Lisa's innocent food-focused assumption

jokes.text: All food tastes like barf now.
jokes.punchline: Blunt description of surgery side effects
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective description of gastric bypass consequences

jokes.text: Slow down, sexy beast. Why don't you take some time to savor the front? What are you hiding from me? Is it chocolate? Uh, it used to be.
jokes.setup: Homer hiding his body from Marge
jokes.punchline: Marge's chocolate guess and Homer's dark response
jokes.explanation: Good body horror humor with chocolate/skin confusion

jokes.text: My implants feel itchy. Yes, I was out of silicone rubber. so I used rolled-up socks.
jokes.punchline: Makeshift implant materials
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Nick incompetence with improvised medical materials

jokes.text: You'd better not make me cry, 'cause I don't know where my tear ducts are anymore.
jokes.punchline: Can't locate basic facial features
jokes.explanation: Good body horror comedy about surgical disfigurement

jokes.text: What...? Why do I look like me again? The doctor called me about all the crazy surgeries you wanted, and I said no.
jokes.punchline: Marge prevented the surgeries
jokes.explanation: Standard resolution revealing it was preventable

jokes.text: The only person who thinks you're a monster is the one who had to give you a sponge bath.
jokes.punchline: Reference to intimate care during recovery
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable intimacy humor about caretaking duties

jokes.text: Plastic surgery is a mistake, because it hasn't been perfected to where you look really good. When it is, everyone should get it. Amen.
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the point of the moral
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - learning completely wrong lesson from experience

jokes.text: Let's face it you could do better. Well, maybe I could. But every time I look at you, I see the same adorable boy I fell in love with.
jokes.punchline: Marge's honest but loving response
jokes.explanation: Sweet relationship moment with gentle humor about their dynamic

jokes.text: Holy heavens! Is that a meteor heading for the Earth? Maybe. But tonight there's a benefit for underpaid comic book artists of the forties and fifties. To the cash bar!
jokes.punchline: Dismissing apocalypse for charity event
jokes.explanation: Good priorities joke - choosing charity over survival

tmdb_overview: Marge opens a gym for real women because she's disenchanted with Springfield's snooty, glitzy L.A. Body Works. Her gym, Shapes: A Gym for Women, is a huge success, launching a chain that makes Marge rich and famous. But Homer fears Marge will leave him for someone more attractive and goes to extreme lengths to try to save his marriage.
